LACHER V. SUPERIOR COURT STANDS FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT A BUILDER WHO SEEKS APPROVAL FROM NEIGHBORS FOR A NEW BUILDING HAS A DUTY NOT TO FRAUDULENTLY MISREPRESENT MATERIAL FACTS OF THE DESIGN IN ORDER TO SECURE APPROVAL/NON-OPPOSITION FROM THOSE NEIGHBORS. THE 3 YEAR SOL ALSO DOES NOT BAR THE CLAIM, BECAUSE P WAS NOT PUT ON REASONABLE NOTICE OF THE ENCROACHMENT UNTIL AUGUST 2006, WHEN THE BUILDING SURVEY WAS CONDUCTED. MOTION IS ALSO DENIED BECAUSE COMPLAINT ALLEGES FACTS THAT IF TRUE COULD SHOW AGENCY RELATIONSHIP. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO 2ND COA - PROMISSORY FRAUD - BECAUSE THIS COA BARRED BY ECONOMIC LOSS RULE. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO 4TH COA, CONTINUOUS NUISANCE FOR WATER DAMAGE, BECAUSE IF IT IS A CONTINUOUS NUISANCE CLAIM IT IS BARRED BY THE 3-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BECAUSE IT DOES NOT RELATE BACK TO THE ORIGINAL FILING OF THE COMPLAINT IN 2009. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT OWNED OR HAD ANY RELATIONSHIP TO THE PROPERTY SINCE 2005; BECAUSE THE CLAIM WAS FILED IN 2013, IT WOULD ONLY REACH BACK TO 2010. Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Staley, Esq. of Epstein, Englert, Staley & Coffey (415-398-2200) for Edward J. Conner, Plaintiff and opposing party through Court call
Paul Samoni, Esq. Law Office of John A Baird (925-746-3957) for Hathaway Dinwiddie Construction Group, Cross Defendant through Court call
Andrew Azarmi, Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge,(415-356-4631)for Cousins/Myers II, LLC, Defendant and moving party for motion for Judgment on Pleadings.
Ari Baruth, Esq. of Long & Levitt LLP (415-438-4596)for Heller Manus Architects, defendant and moving party for Demurrer to 3rd Amended Complaint.

Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs HELLER MANUS ARCHITECTS, JEFFREY HELLER's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Lacher v. Superior Court stands for the proposition that a builder who seeks approval from neighbors for a new building has a duty not to fraudulently misrepresent material facts of the design in order to secure approval/non-opposition from those neighbors. The 3 year SOL also does not bar the claim, because P was not put on reasonable notice of the encroachment until August 2006, when the building survey was conducted. Prevailing party to submit order approved as to form.Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Staley, Esq. of Epstein, Englert, Staley & Coffey (415-398-2200) for Edward J. Conner, Plaintiff and opposing party through Court call
Paul Samoni, Esq. Law Office of John A Baird (925-746-3957) for Hathaway Dinwiddie Construction Group, Cross Defendant through Court call
Andrew Azarmi, Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge,(415-356-4631)for Cousins/Myers II, LLC, Defendant and moving party for motion for Judgment on Pleadings.
Ari Baruth, Esq. of Long & Levitt LLP (415-438-4596)for Heller Manus Architects, defendant and moving party for Demurrer to 3rd Amended Complaint.

Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs COUSINS/MYERS II, LLC, MYERS DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, COUSINS PROPERTIES INCORPORATED, JACK MYERS' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is DENIED as to 3rd and 5th COAs. Lacher v. Superior Court stands for the proposition that a builder who seeks approval from neighbors for a new building has a duty not to fraudulently misrepresent material facts of the design in order to secure approval/non-opposition from those neighbors. The 3 year SOL also does not bar the claim, because P was not put on reasonable notice of the encroachment until August 2006, when the building survey was conducted. Motion is also denied because complaint alleges facts that if true could show agency relationship. Motion is GRANTED as to 2nd COA - promissory fraud - because this COA barred by economic loss rule. Motion is GRANTED as to 4th COA, continuous nuisance for water damage, because if it is a continuous nuisance claim it is barred by the 3-year Statute of Limitations because it does not relate back to the original filing of the complaint in 2009. Defendants have not owned or had any relationship to the property since 2005; because the claim was filed in 2013, it would only reach back to 2010. Prevailing party to submit order approved as to form. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)
